ending: Sievan brings Noah and Wizen together because both carry power connected to Decras, then asks Noah to help him die as a final attempt to escape the limits built into demon-kind. Wizen wounds Sievan and enters the line through the portal Sievan opens, still determined to retrieve Bella from the afterlife. Empty Proliferation protects Noah from Wizen's command. Noah follows Wizen into the line after failing to save the Hollow girl, whose lack of a demon rune had briefly offered a new lead toward curing demons. Zath evacuates Moxie and Lee, leaving them alive but separated from Noah. Lee still has no safe way to create her own Fragment of Self, and Sievan's fate remains unsettled. Yoru and the Web remain in the Damned Plains, where Spider's faction has survived Belkus's opposition and become a significant local power. At Arbitage, Jalen, Brayden, Bird, and Silvertide continue preparing Vermil's students through improvised formations and live-monster exercises. The unresolved grimoire entity, Wizen's pursuit of Bella, and Noah's route back to his companions all carry forward.
in_short: Stranded in the Damned Plains, Noah builds the Web under his Spider identity while researching a cure for Lee. His auction victories draw Yoru, Belkus, and Sievan's agents into his orbit. A lethal experiment produces Warped Matter and reveals the Fragment of Self, while an examination of Igris shows that every demon rune shares a flawed external origin. Noah proposes that Lee create a personal fragment, but no safe catalyst is found. After Noah kills Axil with Sunder and dies himself, Sievan restores her only partly. Belkus trades Noah mind runes for leaving the city, enabling Noah to create Empty Proliferation against Wizen's control. Wizen reaches Sievan to reclaim his dead daughter Bella. Sievan reveals that demons are bound by their rune nature and asks Noah to help him die. Wizen enters the line through Sievan's portal, and Noah follows after the Hollow girl dies despite his healing attempt. Moxie and Lee survive but are evacuated away from him.

recaps.text: Previously: Noah Vines lives under the inherited identity Vermil and teaches Todd, Isabel, Alexandra, James, and Emily at Arbitage. Moxie and Lee are his closest companions, while Brayden is his half-brother and Silvertide has helped train the students. Noah possesses Sunder and has repeatedly survived death through an unusual means of returning to a body. Lee's demon nature and unstable fused runes place her identity and future at risk. Wizen is a powerful enemy whose mind magic and wider plans threaten Noah's group. By the start of this volume, Noah, Moxie, and Lee are separated from their allies in the Damned Plains, where they seek a way to protect Lee and return to the mortal plane.

Spider advances Violet to rank three and appoints her to protect a young knowledge demon who will unite the local gangs. The young leader can taste truth but struggles to control an energy-draining form of feeding, while his shadow-wielding second-in-command serves under a private arrangement. Spider is Noah Vines, and the faction is part of his larger attempt to gather resources in the Damned Plains. Studying these demons may also help him understand Lee's fused and unstable runes. Mascot crosses into the plane, giving Noah, Moxie, and Lee a chance to send a message home, though not a means to leave. They report that they are alive and ask about their students. The newly consolidated street faction adopts the name the Web and prepares for retaliation. Elsewhere, Wizen and Barb establish their own base. Wizen uses mind control to question a captive about Sievan, a demon lord rumored to have returned from the afterlife.

The Web survives an assassination attempt when Moxie and Lee eliminate the attackers. Noah gathers a large stock of low-rank runes, then follows a captured scout to a meeting staged under Pirren's identity. He kills the false figure, forces the real Pirren to negotiate, and secures her help entering an auction. Pirren names Belkus as the ruler of the walking city and mentions Yoku, whose significance remains unclear. Noah's faction is now visible to stronger political interests, while Wizen continues searching separately for Sievan. At Arbitage, Jalen and Brayden supervise Vermil's students as they develop improvised formations. Mascot delivers Noah's letter, providing direct proof that Vermil is alive in the Damned Plains. The message reassures the students and their temporary guardians, but offers no route for reuniting the two groups. A separate Linwick crypt search is also nearing its objective, though its target has not been disclosed.

The Web absorbs the local street lords after Lee destroys a gang that attacked its vulnerable members. Ten gangs now follow Spider, but rapid expansion brings both internal strain and organized retaliation. Pirren's attempt to arrange auction access leads her to Yoku, the Rising Moon, whose future-weighing power detects unusual consequences around Spider's protection. Meanwhile, Bird and Silvertide join Jalen and Brayden in supporting Vermil's students. Bird trades rune assistance for knowledge of their shieldless fighting method, while Alexandra continues training without taking new runes. At the auction, a masked silver-haired demon introduces herself as Yoru and is confirmed to be Yoku. She prepares a poisoned drink as a test, but every likely future in which Spider or his companions consume it ends in her death. Unable to explain why Spider creates such dangerous outcomes, Yoru abandons the poison and changes her objective from controlling him to securing an alliance. The auction then begins with Noah seeking material for Lee's runes and his own advancement.

Noah turns the auction into a public demonstration of Spider's power. He kills Matis, defeats Sinclaire, acquires useful matter runes, and buys the broken death-associated Master Rune Deathly Night. The purchase may attract Sievan, who normally claims valuable death runes. Yoru buys an additional Unstable Mass rune as a gift for Noah and reveals through her choices that her future sight cannot reliably resolve him. Her Master Rune, Moonlit Prophecy, normally destroys the minds of those who meet her gaze, but it collapses when used on Spider. The encounter leaves her convinced that she cannot place him under her control and must instead become part of his plans. Noah still does not understand her full purpose, but his auction victories greatly increase Spider's wealth and reputation. His immediate gains support two linked goals: building enough power to oppose Wizen and finding a way to reconstruct Lee's dangerous runes without destroying who she is.

Yoru joins the Web's camp after her failed attempt to read Spider's future. Noah enforces a rule that she cannot take from weaker camp members, and Violet cautiously shares a tent with her. Yoru can predict likely words and actions so precisely that it resembles mind reading, but she has little understanding of friendship, love, failure, or mutual loyalty. Violet tries to teach her that people cannot be treated solely as tools, while Yoru admits that no future in which she opposes Spider ends in victory. Her deeper aim is freedom from anyone else's control, and Noah advises her that genuine desires must be discovered through experience rather than copied. At Arbitage, Isabel becomes interested in fighting without sight as the students continue demanding combat exercises under Jalen and Silvertide. In the Damned Plains, the Web remains wary of Yoru, but she stays with the faction and begins questioning a life governed entirely by survival and prediction.

Noah attempts to forge a new matter rune by combining two auction purchases with power copied from Crumbling Space. He uses his own death and bodily reconstruction as the catalyst, badly cracking his soul in the process. The experiment unexpectedly creates two conjoined structures: Warped Matter and a Fragment of Self. A temporary duplicate within his mind helps him recognize that the fragment is not an ordinary rune, but the connection joining his body, soul, desires, and magic. Reintegrating it gives Noah far finer control over his body and frees Warped Matter, whose branching effect consumes nearby material until its limited charge runs out. The resemblance between Noah's discovery and Lee's bound demon runes offers a promising direction, but Noah refuses to repeat the dangerous method on her. Lee's seal remains stable for a limited time under restrained use. As Noah prepares to study hostile demons directly, Sievan orders an unnamed subordinate to retrieve a particular rune, bringing a greater power's attention toward Spider.

Wizen infiltrates the City of Gold with Barb and begins gathering information about Sievan. In the walking city, Noah and Moxie attack Igris, the rank-five wealth demon believed to have funded assaults on the Web. Moxie's new flowering vine consumes energy throughout the mansion while Noah forces a mind-meld with Igris. Inside the demon's soul, Noah discovers that different demon runes all reduce to the same foreign rank-one foundation. Igris dies from the damage, but the discovery shows that Lee's problem is not unique. Every demon appears to carry an imitation derived from the same outside being. Noah proposes replacing that dependence by helping Lee create her own Fragment of Self. She needs intent, power, and a deeply personal catalyst, but the group cannot identify a safe catalyst and Noah refuses self-mutilation as a method. Their new theory gives Lee a direction without yet providing a cure. At the same time, Axil begins hunting Spider on Sievan's behalf for the death-associated Master Rune.

Belkus sends four rank-five enforcers to execute Spider for Igris's death, but Axil kills them and demands a fight of her own. Her extreme regeneration defeats Noah's conventional attacks, so he uses Sunder. The strike kills both of them. Noah returns to life with his magic temporarily sealed, while Sievan intercepts Axil after death and restores her at a steep cost. Sunder has left only part of her body, strength, and runes intact. Noah refuses her demand for Deathly Night and reframes the dispute as a negotiation between Spider and Sievan. The public deaths and returns elevate Spider's reputation and force Belkus to reconsider direct hostility. Instead of launching another assault, Belkus has Zorin, his commander, request Spider's presence. Noah faces a diminished Axil, lacks access to his magic for several more hours, and needs leverage against both demon lords while the search for Lee's personal Fragment of Self remains stalled.

Axil identifies Noah's grimoire as a living abomination made from fused demon bodies and damaged souls. Her fear gives Noah bargaining leverage, but a hidden presence associated with the book threatens her into silence. Sievan's unnamed harbinger arrives to negotiate as Zorin escorts Noah, Yoru, and the others to Belkus. In the audience, Belkus recognizes Yoru as Yoku, the Rising Moon, and accuses her of manipulating people to seize his city. Yoru leaves with Mascot after securing the future she wants. Noah then reveals that the Web's information network exists to find Wizen and that he is a teacher rather than a would-be ruler. Belkus recognizes Wizen's name, creating a possible route to the enemy Noah has been seeking. The meeting also clarifies the balance around Spider: Belkus wants him removed from Yoru's influence, Sievan wants his rune, and Yoru regards joining his side as her only viable future.

Belkus and Zath confirm that Wizen is in the City of Gold, where he is killing powerful demons and openly challenging Sievan. Noah agrees to leave Belkus's city and not support Yoru against him, receiving several mind runes in return. He dismantles them with Sunder and refuses concepts based on domination or direct mental harm. Drawing on the purposeless repetition he endured on the line, he constructs Empty Proliferation, a flawless rank-four mind rune intended to resist Wizen and perhaps disrupt his control over others. Zath waits to take Noah, Moxie, and Lee to Sievan while conversing with an invisible sapient presence that may be tied to the grimoire. At Arbitage, Todd, Isabel, Alexandra, James, and Emily continue training under Jalen, Brayden, Silvertide, and another combat instructor, progressing to live-monster formation work. Both groups are preparing for confrontations: the students for their examination, and Noah for an enemy who can overwhelm demon lords.

Wizen reaches Sievan's domain with a young demon guide whose failing soul cannot hold runes. He protects her during the ascent and finally reveals his purpose: his daughter Bella is dead, and he believes Sievan can open a route to the afterlife so he can reclaim her. When Sievan refuses, Wizen draws on the key and several bound powers to attack. Zath portals Noah, Moxie, and Lee into the confrontation. Renewal and Decras observe that Sievan deliberately allowed Wizen through so the two intruders would meet. Decras reveals that he created Sievan, the first demon, from part of his own runes. Even with seven perfected rank-eight runes, Sievan cannot advance beyond the nature imposed on him. He explains privately to Noah that demons literally are their flawed runes and will eventually be consumed by them. The young guide is Hollow, with no rune tether at all, and is dying. Sievan admits that he too is dying and asks Noah to help him die, hoping death might offer a final path beyond his limits.

Noah recognizes that Wizen's defining rune is Weave, not Sunder. It combines powers taken or bound from other mages, explaining the range of magic Wizen can wield. Noah orders Zath to evacuate Moxie and Lee, separating himself from them as Wizen continues attacking Sievan. The Hollow girl confirms that both Wizen and Noah want Sievan dead. Because she has no demon rune, Noah briefly sees her as a possible unaltered baseline who might reveal how to free Lee and demon-kind from rune compulsion. Wizen wounds Sievan and tries to command Noah, but Empty Proliferation prevents the order from taking hold. Sievan opens a portal to the line, giving Wizen the route he needs to pursue Bella in the afterlife. Noah attempts to save the Hollow girl with Renewal's fragment, but she dies before he can learn from her. Wizen enters the portal, and Noah follows him. Moxie and Lee survive with Zath, while Lee's cure, Sievan's desired death, the grimoire's hidden entity, and Noah's return route all remain unresolved.
